text.title: decimal-as-string-pattern Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for decimal-as-string-pattern. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: For currency, exact decimals, big integers (greater than 2^53), and structured identifiers, declare the schema field as a string with a regex pattern that pins the exact surface form, then parse to Decimal/int/datetime on the receiving side.
rule.rationale: JSON number types do not pin precision, and float deserialization can introduce binary-rounding drift. Regex-constrained strings are masked at decode time on strict-mode or grammar backends — the model literally cannot emit "19.989999" when the pattern demands two decimal places.

rule.statement: Pick the strictest pattern that the legitimate input distribution can satisfy; pair it with a Field description that names the format and gives one canonical example.
rule.rationale: Patterns are enforced at decode time on strict mode or constrained backends, but the model still needs the description as a prompt anchor on JSON-mode-only fallbacks. The pattern catches drift; the description teaches the format on the first try.
